prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is in black and white and focuses on Listerine Tooth Paste.

It opens with a close-up of a tube of **“LISTERINE TOOTH PASTE”** held horizontally in someone’s hands. The person appears to be wearing a light-colored coat or jacket, but their face is not shown.

The next shots show people brushing their teeth. First, a young boy is shown in close-up, holding a toothbrush to his mouth and brushing while looking slightly to the side. Then a young girl or young woman faces the camera and smiles while brushing. Large white text reading **“LISTERI…”** appears over the lower part of this frame, partially covering her and the toothbrush. A following shot shows another young woman in profile or three-quarter view, also smiling while brushing her teeth.

The visual emphasis then shifts toward the product packaging. A woman continues brushing in the foreground while a Listerine Tooth Paste package appears prominently beside or behind her, creating a layered product-and-user composition.

A close-up product shot follows, showing boxed packaging labeled **“LISTERINE TOOTH PASTE.”** A large price graphic on the front reads **“ONLY 59¢”**, and smaller text appears to mention **“Two 45¢ Tubes,”** though the lower text is partly unclear due to image quality.

The final frame shows a person in a light-colored coat standing behind a counter or display area with multiple Listerine Tooth Paste packages arranged around them. The central package display again shows the **59¢** price. The person’s face is not visible, and they appear to be handling or gesturing toward an object on the counter, but the object is not clear from the sampled frame.
